summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Furniva e-commerce platform. Fixed critical bug (sku field required in Order model). 19/19 backend API tests passed (100%). All frontend UI tests passed including Login, Inventory page (Master SKU, Platform Listings, Procurement modals), Returns Management with status filtering, Costing page with KPIs, and Order Detail page with Timeline and Financial Calculation modal.

minor.issue: Orders imported without 'sku' field were causing 500 errors. FIXED: Made sku and product_name optional in models.py

ui_bugs.issue: Modal close button (×) has overlay interception issue - clicking takes 30s timeout. Using force=True helps but not ideal

issues: Many orders show 'Unknown' customer and ₹0 amount - these appear to be imported orders with incomplete data

critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: models.py - sku field was required but imported orders may not have it. Changed to Optional[str] = None
FIXED: models.py - product_name was required but imported orders may not have it. Changed to Optional[str] = None
Minor: Multiple React Hook useEffect dependency warnings in frontend console - not blocking but should be addressed
seed_data_creation: Test data exists: Master SKU 'TEST-001' with 1 listing and 1 procurement batch, order 'TEST-ORD-001' with return request
context_for_next_testing_agent: All core features working: Auth, Dashboard with stats, Inventory page (Master SKU CRUD, Platform Listings modal, Procurement Batches modal with weighted average cost), Returns Management (list, filter by status, detail modal with status update), Costing page (4 KPIs, How It Works section), Order Detail (all cards present: Order Info, Timeline, Customer, Communication checklist, Quick Actions, Return Info). Financial calculation modal works. 126 orders in system (most are imported Amazon orders with incomplete data). Navigation sidebar has all 10 items.

features_tested.inventory_page: PASS - Master SKU cards display, Add Master SKU modal opens with all fields (name, product, category, amazon/flipkart SKUs)
features_tested.platform_listings_modal: PASS - Opens for TEST-001, shows existing listing (AMZ-SKU-001), can add new listings with platform selector
features_tested.procurement_modal: PASS - Opens for TEST-001, shows existing batch, displays weighted avg cost, can add new batch with qty/unit cost
features_tested.inventory_edit_delete: PASS via API - Edit and delete Master SKU tested in backend tests
features_tested.order_detail_page: PASS - Shows all fields (SKU, ASIN, channel, pricing), Timeline card, Communication checklist, Quick Actions
features_tested.create_return_from_order: PASS via API - Create return modal tested (not visible on UI for order with existing return)
features_tested.calculate_financials: PASS - Modal opens with all cost fields, calculates profit correctly
features_tested.returns_page: PASS - Returns list displays, status filter works, Manage button opens detail modal with status update options
features_tested.costing_page: PASS - Shows 4 KPIs (Total Revenue ₹90,000, Net Revenue ₹73,800, Total Costs ₹56,000, Gross Profit ₹17,800), How It Works section present
features_tested.navigation_sidebar: PASS - All 10 items present: Dashboard, Orders, Inventory, Returns, Costing, Channels, Tasks, Analytics, WhatsApp, Team

rca_of_issue: The /api/orders/ endpoint was returning 500 Internal Server Error because many imported orders in the database were missing the 'sku' field. The Order model in models.py had 'sku: str' as a required field. This was fixed by changing to 'sku: Optional[str] = None' and 'product_name: Optional[str] = None'.
